sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 3.7 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.3)

statement.latex: An automorphism$$of a group$G$is called algebraic\/ if, for any$g G$, the minimal$$-inva\-ri\-ant subgroup of$G$containing$g$is finitely generated. An automorphism$$of$G$is called an$e$-auto\-mor\-phism\/ if, for any$$-inva\-ri\-ant subgroups$A$and$B$, where$A$is a proper subgroup of$B$,$A B$contains an element$x$such that$[x, ] B$. Is every$e$-auto\-mor\-phism algebraic? { V.\,G.\,Vilyatser} Yes,...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
